name: Pyodide (Python in the Browser via WebAssembly)

auth.notes: Client-side browser execution; no server auth required

performance.notes: Cold start 5-10MB WASM download; subsequent runs fast; slower than native CPython

agent_readiness.notes: CPython compiled to WebAssembly for in-browser Python. Browser/HTML: <script src='pyodide.js'>. JS: pyodide = await loadPyodide(). pyodide.runPython('import numpy as np; np.sum([1,2,3])'). await pyodide.loadPackage('numpy'). Python/JS interop: from js import document; document.getElementById('out').textContent = 'Hello'. pyodide.globals.get('my_var'). Packages: numpy, pandas, scipy, matplotlib, scikit-learn, 250+ packages. micropip: import micropip; await micropip.install('requests'). Worker: use Web Worker for non-blocking. JupyterLite is Pyodide-based. PyScript framework builds on Pyodide.

gotchas: 5-10MB WASM download on cold start — not suitable for low-bandwidth scenarios
No network access from Python inside browser (CORS, WebSocket only)
Many C extensions not available — pure Python or compiled-for-WASM packages only
Performance 1.5-3x slower than native CPython for compute-heavy tasks
File system access limited — uses virtual FS, not real disk
best_when: Interactive Python in browser: educational notebooks, live code demos
Client-side data processing without server (privacy-preserving analytics)
Serverless Python execution in browser for cost reduction
avoid_when: When package availability is critical (limited WASM-compiled packages)
summary: Pyodide runs CPython in the browser via WebAssembly. loadPyodide() → pyodide.runPython(). loadPackage('numpy'). micropip.install() for pure-Python packages. JS/Python interop: from js import document. 250+ pre-compiled packages. JupyterLite/PyScript built on it. MPL-2.0 by Mozilla/Pyodide contributors. Best for interactive browser Python (education, demos). 5-10MB cold start. No real network in Python. 1.5-3x slower than native.
